avoid: Replacing cuBLAS for W @ X with a custom GEMM kernel.
Unsafe caching by shape alone or pointer alone without version counters.
Hardcoding hidden dimensions or assuming fixed tensor addresses.
evidence: Aggregate speedup is 1.01x, essentially identical to torch baseline.
Latency per shape nearly matches torch reference, indicating no meaningful optimization.
Profile summary identifies multiple ATen GEMM and add launches as the bottleneck.
No custom kernel or fusion is applied; the candidate is a pure ATen reference.

recommended_actions: fuse rank-16 update into the output tensor
test scalar and vectorized rank-16 kernels
Fuse the rank-16 update (A @ (B.T @ X)) and the addition into a single kernel to reduce launch overhead.
Keep cuBLAS for the full W @ X matmul; do not attempt a custom full GEMM.
Explore a final-output cache keyed by data_ptr, version_counter, d, and device if inputs are static across calls, but only with strict correctness guards.
